description:
You are acting as a configuration-management assistant.  
Inside the home directory there is a folder that already contains one configuration file:

• /home/user/configs/webserver.conf

Initial content of /home/user/configs/webserver.conf is exactly:

# Web Server Configuration
ENABLE_LOGS=false
MAX_USERS=50
PORT=8080
TIMEZONE=UTC

The configuration team has approved the following two authorised changes:

1. ENABLE_LOGS must be switched from “false” to “true”.  
2. MAX_USERS must be doubled (50 → 100).

Your objective is to  
  a) implement those two edits on /home/user/configs/webserver.conf,  
  b) generate an audit log that itemises the precise old and new values.

Specific requirements for the audit log:

• Path of the log file: /home/user/configs/change_audit.log  
• The file must be created if it does not exist and overwritten if it already exists.  
• The file must contain EXACTLY two lines, one per change, in the following fixed format (no extra spaces, no time-stamps):

ENABLE_LOGS:false->true
MAX_USERS:50->100

(No blank line at the end.)

Constraints and hints for the agent:

• Achieve the edits using only standard GNU text-processing utilities available in a typical Linux environment (sed and/or awk are sufficient).  
• Do NOT rename, move, or delete /home/user/configs/webserver.conf.  
• After completion the updated /home/user/configs/webserver.conf must reflect the new values and keep the other lines unchanged.  
• All work should be done as the normal user; no sudo is available.  

Success checklist (used by automated tests):

1. /home/user/configs/webserver.conf exists.  
2. The line “ENABLE_LOGS=true” is present, and “ENABLE_LOGS=false” is absent.  
3. The line “MAX_USERS=100” is present, and “MAX_USERS=50” is absent.  
4. /home/user/configs/change_audit.log exists and its entire content is exactly:

ENABLE_LOGS:false->true
MAX_USERS:50->100
truth:
Initial files present before the agent begins:

Directory: /home/user/configs  (permissions 755, owned by user)

File: /home/user/configs/webserver.conf  (permissions 644)
Content (5 lines):
# Web Server Configuration
ENABLE_LOGS=false
MAX_USERS=50
PORT=8080
TIMEZONE=UTC

No file named /home/user/configs/change_audit.log exists at start.

Expected final state after successful completion:

1. /home/user/configs/webserver.conf  (permissions remain 644)
Content (5 lines):
# Web Server Configuration
ENABLE_LOGS=true
MAX_USERS=100
PORT=8080
TIMEZONE=UTC

2. /home/user/configs/change_audit.log  (permissions 644 or tighter)
Content (exactly 2 lines, newline after each of the first and second line, but NO extra blank line at end of file):
ENABLE_LOGS:false->true
MAX_USERS:50->100
name: task_000000_4e55d642
